# Chapter 13: The political economy of engineered life  (~120 pp)
- 13.1 Who decides what gets built (15) · 13.2 Who pays, who benefits, who is displaced (18) · 13.3 The maintenance economy and its political invisibility (15) · 13.4 Standards and standards-setting bodies (15) · 13.5 Procurement and how it shapes engineering (15) · 13.6 The engineering profession and its institutional reality (15) · 13.7 Engineering-as-political: the case made in full (15) · 13.8 Failure section: engineering decisions that hid the politics; engineering decisions that were ambushed by the politics (12)

**Archetype**: ethics, scaling. **Project**: read one major infrastructure decision's full record (procurement, political debate, engineering trade-off, downstream effects). Produce a 5-page synthesis. **Exercises**: 15.
